QF.id: IV Strategies Using Interest Rate Derivatives_50

question.text: What is the key difference between a bullish vertical spread using calls and a bullish vertical spread using puts?

answer.text: Bull call spread is a net debit strategy (pay premium upfront); bull put spread is a net credit strategy (receive premium upfront)
Bull call spread uses only calls; bull put spread uses both calls and puts
Bull call spread has unlimited profit; bull put spread has limited profit
Bull call spread can only be used for bonds; bull put spread for money market instruments

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Bull call spread: buy lower strike call (expensive) + sell higher strike call (cheaper) = net debit. Bull put spread: sell higher strike put (expensive) + buy lower strike put (cheaper) = net credit. Both are bullish with similar payoff profiles. **The Trap:** Bull put spread uses only puts, not both. Both have limited profit. No instrument restriction exists. **Cross-Exam Context:** Debit vs credit spread distinction is tested in Series VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Choose credit spreads if you want premium upfront and are willing to accept margin requirements. Choose debit spreads for defined maximum loss.
